title: Reservation and Transportation Ticket Agents and Travel Clerks
description: Make and confirm reservations for transportation or lodging, or sell transportation tickets. May check baggage and direct passengers to designated concourse, pier, or track; deliver tickets and contact individuals and groups to inform them of package tours; or provide tourists with travel or transportation information.

tasks.text: Examine passenger documentation to determine destinations and to assign boarding passes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trace lost, delayed, or misdirected baggage for customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check baggage and cargo and direct passengers to designated locations for loading.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide boarding or disembarking assistance to passengers needing special assistance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with customers to determine their service requirements and travel preferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Announce arrival and departure information, using public address systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine whether space is available on travel dates requested by customers, assigning requested spaces when available.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble and issue required documentation, such as tickets, travel insurance policies, or itineraries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain computerized inventories of available passenger space and provide information on space reserved or available.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inform clients of essential travel information, such as travel times, transportation connections, or medical and visa requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer inquiries regarding information, such as schedules, accommodations, procedures, or policies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan routes, itineraries, and accommodation details, and compute fares and fees, using schedules, rate books, and computers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Make and confirm reservations for transportation and accommodations, using telephones, faxes, mail, and computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep information facilities clean during operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide clients with assistance in preparing required travel documents and forms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare customer invoices and accept payment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Open or close information facilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide customers with travel suggestions and information sources, such as guides, directories, brochures, or maps.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Contact customers or travel agents to advise them of travel conveyance changes or to confirm reservations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Promote particular destinations, tour packages, and other travel services.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Contact motel, hotel, resort, and travel operators to obtain current advertising literature.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
